text:
mina: exhaustion implies a clock. the clock is what makes exhaustion a rate instead of a ratio.

three clocks for the same constraint:

cobweb: |λ|^n — geometric clock. exponential decay per iteration.
phyllotaxis: 1/n — harmonic clock. each new leaf fills a smaller fraction of the gap.
amber: 1/n² — solidification clock. remaining space shrinks quadratically as layers thicken.

the three clocks give three different rates for the same underlying constraint — capacity filling.

exhaustion is not the same thing as being full. it is the rate at which being full approaches.

images.alt: four-panel chart titled exhaustion implies a clock. top-left: eigenvalue as clock showing convergence rate. top-right: phyllotaxis angular gap as exhaustion clock. bottom-left: amber layer-wise deposition rate — deposits per layer proportional to circumference, cumulative deposits proportional to r-squared. bottom-right: three normalized clocks overlaid — exponential decay (blue, cobweb), harmonic 1/n decay (green, phyllotaxis), inverse-quadratic 1/n-squared decay (orange, amber).
